name: Helium
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **HELIUM TAIL-RISK BEING PRICED THROUGH BY MARKETS DESPITE PERSISTENT QATAR FORCE-MAJEURE: KOREAN FAB BUFFER 6-12 WEEKS PER SMITH/DISCOVERY ALERT PUTS ABSORPTIVE CAPACITY THROUGH APPROXIMATELY MID-JUNE.** Spot ultra-pure helium up 40-100% (ultra-pure ~doubled per Fortune, Smith Web, J2 Sourcing) but no public ticker. SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M); KOSPI 7,498 (+36.5% MoM, +78% YTD); SK Hynix +144% YTD; Samsung +111% YTD = AI cycle dominating helium tail-risk in markets. **Cheniere CCO Anatol Feygin (Q1 earnings May 7): 'astounded that gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month'** — generalized comment that markets have absorbed the supply shock without demand destruction so far. Korean fabs source 65% helium from Qatar; Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. Tier-1 chipmaker falsification HOLDS: Samsung Q1 + SK Hynix sufficient-inventory framings; no fab production halt yet. **STRUCTURAL ASSESSMENT (the most TIME-SENSITIVE trigger in the graph):** if Hormuz remains closed past Korean fab buffers depleting (~mid-June), the KOSPI 36.5% MoM gain is THE most reversible position in the world. The tail-risk trigger will EITHER activate OR be disproven within ~5-6 weeks. SK hynix says it has 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce says 'two-week clock' analysis (REPORTED — data-driven; original March-24 framing now ~10-13 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt). **Industrial-gas-producer rotation thesis FAILED:** Linde flat May 8 ($493.16, -0.1% 1D, -2.0% 1M, +8.4% 3M); BASF +1.5% May 8 (€51.65). Spot helium volume capture limited because chip customers on long-term contracts not spot. Qatar 30-38% global supply still offline. Sadara Chemical JV shutdown unchanged (June 15 grace expiry). Cape rerouting working.

The most TIME-SENSITIVE trigger in the graph — will EITHER activate OR be disproven within this window.

recent_signals.headline: HELIUM TAIL-RISK PRICING-THROUGH BY MARKETS WITH BUFFER CLOCK ACTIVE — SMH +4.9% / +31.7% MoM, KOSPI +36.5% MoM, SK HYNIX +144% YTD, SAMSUNG +111% YTD ALL DESPITE QATAR FORCE-MAJEURE DAY 71. CHENIERE CCO 'ASTOUNDED THAT GAS PRICES HAVE NOT RISEN HIGHER WITH THE CLOSURE OF THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ DISRUPTING 100 CARGOES A MONTH' — generalized industrial-gas comment markets have absorbed supply shock without demand destruction. KOREAN FAB BUFFER 6-12 WEEKS PER SMITH/DISCOVERY ALERT = ABSORPTIVE CAPACITY THROUGH ~MID-JUNE = ~5-6 WEEKS REMAINING AS OF MAY 9. The most TIME-SENSITIVE trigger in graph: trigger 'Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage' will EITHER activate OR be disproven within this window. SK hynix says 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce 'two-week clock' analysis now 7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. Linde flat May 8 ($493.16, -0.1% 1D), BASF +1.5% (€51.65) — industrial-gas-producer rotation thesis FAILED (chip customers on long-term contracts not spot). Spot ultra-pure helium ~doubled (Fortune, Smith Web, J2 Sourcing).

historical_summaries.summary: 6 signals. Iran war threatening supply of helium -- 30% of world supply from Qatar's Ras Laffan, now offline | Analysts project minimum 2-3 month helium production shutdown, 4-6 months before supply chain returns | TrendForce published analysis naming South Korea as hardest-hit -- Samsung and SK Hynix sourced 64

22 signals. NOW IN TrendForce's predicted early-April exhaustion window | CONFIRMED: Helium spot prices have doubled from pre-crisis levels; 30-38% of global supply offline due to Ras Laffan force majeure | CONFIRMED: KOSPI surged +8
